summary: Meson/compile checks failed during the build: test compile/link commands produced errors (missing headers, pragma diagnostics turned into errors, and linker failures), causing the CI job to fail before tests ran.
root_cause: Meson attempted to compile/check small programs and compilation failed with missing-header errors and diagnostic-as-error pragmas (e.g. missing 'version' and 'sys/mman.h' headers and '-Wattributes' promoted to error), leading to linker failures and aborting the build.

evidence.reason: Shows the configure/compile check failed because a required header 'version' was not found.
Shows another missing system header detected during compilation checks.
Indicates that attribute-related warnings are being treated as errors, which will turn warnings into hard compilation failures.

evidence.quote: collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
evidence.reason: Shows a linker error occurred during compilation of a test/program, causing the build to fail.
Demonstrates that the C compiler was invoked by Meson to build test programs and that the failure happened during those invocations.

suggested_fix: Install or make available the missing system/development headers (e.g. provide headers like sys/mman.h and any 'version' header) and/or adjust the build/meson configuration so attribute warnings (e.g. -Wattributes) are not promoted to errors; then re-run the build.
